toolInput.old_string:
## The Hook

> My Mac Mini is my daily driver. The DGX Spark under my desk is my AI backend. They're connected by three things: Tailscale, Samba, and a half-baked script called lmswitch.
>
> Here's how the whole thing works 👇
toolInput.new_string:
## The Hook

> My Mac Mini is my daily driver. The DGX Spark under my desk is my AI backend. Everything's wired together with Samba, SSH, and a tiny script called lmswitch.
>
> Here's the setup 👇

toolInput.file_path: /home/jvr0x/vaults/universe/06_Projects/Spark Model Publishing/X Post — Mac Mini Daily Driver + Spark AI Backend.md
toolInput.old_string:
[Mac Mini] daily driver
  ├── SMB mount: browse Spark's vault in Finder
  ├── Tailscale SSH: passwordless remote access
  └── OpenCode: connects to Spark's llama-server over Tailscale

The glue:
  • lmswitch — toggle models on Spark → auto-regenerates opencode.json → rsyncs to Mac
  • Tailscale (userspace + SOCKS5) — zero-config private network
  • Samba — file-level access from macOS

Tailscale runs in userspace mode (not GUI app) so it doesn't fight Mullvad VPN.
That means no MagicDNS — just raw 100.x IPs and a SOCKS5 proxy at localhost:1055.

Spark → Mac SSH config uses ProxyCommand through the SOCKS5:
  Host death-star
    User dev
    ProxyCommand /usr/bin/nc -X 5 -x localhost:1055 %h %p

The weird part? The Mac doesn't even know the Spark exists until I SSH into it.
No Bonjour. No mDNS. Just a 100.x IP and an ed25519 key.
That's the whole connection.
toolInput.new_string:
[Mac Mini] daily driver
  ├── SMB mount: browse Spark's vault in Finder (local LAN)
  ├── SSH: connects to Spark over LAN by hostname
  ├── OpenCode: points to Spark's llama-server on the LAN
  └── Config sync: opencode.json, grok config, and hermes config all come from the Spark

When I'm away from home, I use Tailscale (userspace mode) to SSH into the Spark
from my phone — or I trigger it via Hermes/Telegram. No GUI app, doesn't fight
Mullvad. Just a SOCKS5 proxy at localhost:1055 and a ProxyCommand in SSH config.

The glue:
  • lmswitch — toggle models on Spark → auto-regenerates opencode.json → syncs to Mac
  • Samba — file-level access from macOS
  • Config sync — everything's glued: same opencode/grok/hermes configs pointing
    at the Spark's hostname and port on both machines

Spark → Mac SSH config (when on Tailscale, away from home):
  Host death-star
    User dev
    ProxyCommand /usr/bin/nc -X 5 -x localhost:1055 %h %p

toolInput.old_string:
## Thread Beats

1. **Main post** — hook + high-level story (Mac Mini + Spark + "three things connect them")
2. **Pinned reply** — architecture diagram, how lmswitch works, the Tailscale + Mullvad gotcha, SSH config
3. **Optional follow-up** — why this setup matters (local AI, zero cloud dependency, agent lives under my desk)
toolInput.new_string:
## Thread — full post

**Tweet 1 (hook)**

My Mac Mini is my daily driver. Under my desk sits a DGX Spark running LLMs locally.

No cloud. No API calls.

Here's the setup 👇

**Tweet 2**

The Spark is the brain. It runs 7 llama.cpp models (Qwen3.6, Gemma, Nemotron) plus 4 vLLM servers with NVFP4 and Blackwell tensor cores.

The Mac is the client. I SSH into the Spark by hostname over LAN, mount its vault via Samba so I can browse files in Finder, and point OpenCode at its llama-server.

**Tweet 3**

The glue is a tiny script called lmswitch. I toggle models on the Spark → it auto-regenerates opencode.json → syncs the config to the Mac.

So both machines have the same opencode, grok, and hermes configs — same hostname, same port, everything's glued together.

**Tweet 4**

When I'm away from home, I still need access. So I use Tailscale in userspace mode to SSH into the Spark from my phone. Or I just hit Hermes/Telegram and tell my agents to do stuff.

No GUI app — it'd fight with Mullvad. Just a SOCKS5 proxy and a ProxyCommand in SSH config.

**Tweet 5**

This matters because my AI runs on hardware I own, sitting under my desk, connected to my network. My agent knows me because it lives in my house — not because a company collected my data.

Pinned reply below has the full architecture + SSH config 🔽

## Alternative Thread (shorter version)

**Tweet 1 (hook)**

My Mac Mini is my daily driver. Under my desk sits a DGX Spark running LLMs locally.

No cloud. No API calls.

Here's the setup 👇

**Tweet 2**

The Spark runs 7 LLMs, 4 vLLM servers, and a Telegram bot. The Mac mounts the vault via Samba, SSH's in over LAN, and points OpenCode at the Spark's llama-server.

Everything's tied together by a tiny script called lmswitch that syncs configs.

**Tweet 3**

Away from home? Tailscale (userspace mode, doesn't fight Mullvad) lets me SSH from my phone or trigger agents via Hermes/Telegram.

My AI runs on hardware I own. My agent knows me because it lives under my desk, not because a company collected my data.
